summary:
Machine learning methods outperform statistical methods in survival analysis when dealing with high-dimensional, non-linear data and complex patient profiles common in modern clinical practice. They improve early risk stratification and model performance compared to traditional techniques like Cox proportional hazards models, though limitations related to data bias and quality persist. 

Note: This summary is based solely on the provided text content of Agent2’s response; no external information was used.
